meta.name: Step-wise GRPO
meta.problem_solved: Memory operations produce sparse, discontinuous rewards across a fragmented multi-stage trajectory, so terminal task reward doesn't cleanly attribute to the memory decisions that caused it.
meta.naive_baseline: Vanilla GRPO assumes a continuous trajectory and scores the rollout as one unit.
meta.how_it_works: Compute the terminal reward R(τ) then broadcast it to all preceding steps so each intermediate step (incl. cross-stage memory actions) receives the same advantage A_t = A_T — long-range credit assignment across stage boundaries.
meta.input: multi-stage trajectory τ = (τ1, τ2, τ3) with terminal reward
meta.output: per-step advantages for heterogeneous memory + reasoning actions
meta.tradeoff: Uniform broadcast is coarse — every step gets identical credit, which can over-reward incidental actions on a successful rollout.
meta.reusable_pattern: When rewards are terminal but decisions are spread across phases, broadcast the terminal advantage to all steps rather than discounting — cheap long-range credit assignment.
